page: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dual Intel Xeon 8259CL processors. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

A key feature of the m5n.metal is its 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, which supports high-throughput data transfers. Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 82015.21 and a Passmark CPU score of 57124.09. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 219.15 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 95.5 GB/sec for large block write operations. The server also demonstrates strong database capabilities, achieving 77.84 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and scoring 21823.39 in Passmark database operations.

While the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and static web serving, its single-core performance is moderate, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1334.0. Additionally, without dedicated GPU hardware, it is inefficient for large language model text generation, yielding only 5.26 tokens per second on medium 7B models. Given its high resource density, dedicated bare-metal access, and 100 Gbps networking, the m5n.metal is highly cost-efficient for network-bound workloads, large-scale database deployments, containerized microservices, and high-concurrency web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Optimized for high-speed networking, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds reaching 219.15 GB/sec. It performs exceptionally well in database operations, achieving 77.84 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. However, single-core performance is average, and it is inefficient for LLM text generation due to the lack of GPU acceleration. This server is best suited for network-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and high-concurrency web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput database and network-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, ideal for databases and web serving.
tagline: A high-bandwidth bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs and 100 Gbps networking for demanding database and network-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM on a dedicated bare-metal platform
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
Strong multi-core performance and high-speed memory bandwidth
Excellent database throughput with 77.84 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or GPU acceleration, making it unsuitable for heavy AI inference
